Anthropic signed a multiyear deal with $CRWV to rent compute capacity for building and deploying its Claude models.

It is another sign that AI model demand is still pulling hard on third-party infrastructure.
SpaceX reportedly generated ~$19B in revenue last year while losing just under $5B.

Anthropic is exploring designing its own AI chips as rising Claude demand and industry-wide GPU shortages push leading labs to gain more control over compute.

Plans are still early but it shows how intense the race for AI infrastructure has become.

That is notable because Anthropic is limiting Mythos due to cybersecurity risk & high compute costs which says a lot about the kind of model Amazon’s chips are now helping train.

📺 “MIND THE GAP”: A STRESS TEST FOR THIS RALLY

The market made a sharp move higher from ~9.8% off the highs straight up to ~$677 $SPY area.

This move was technically supported by an inverted head-and-shoulders pattern, signaling a potential reversal, and by strength, as it rose even while oil was rising (normally a headwind).

So, again, this move was not driven by ceasefire news, but by both technical and behavioral confirmation.

*

The entire strategy right now revolves around one key level:
– $SPY gap level: ~$671.50
– $QQQ gap level: ~$602

This gap came from the ceasefire news catalyst.

Why does this gap matter? Think of it like a stress test for the rally.

Scenario A — Bullish (Best Case) 
– Price holds above the gap
– Market goes sideways (“digests”)
– Overbought conditions cool off (oscillator was ~+60)

This builds a strong base → higher probability of continuation up.

Scenario B — Bearish / Caution 
– Price breaks below the gap
– Market starts filling the gap (pullback)

That opens the door to short-term downside trades and new support/resistance levels forming.

This behaves like a post-earnings gap: hold it → trend continues, lose it → volatility + reversal risk.

*

What to watch today? Look for price behavior clues, not predictions.

Constructive action:
– Market dips → then recovers (e.g., from red to green)
– Holds above gap → goes sideways
This = healthy digestion

Weak action:
– Break below gap → stays below
– Starts filling the gap aggressively
This = loss of momentum

*

So, if you were long yesterday, sell strength. If you are long today = potentially buy the dip.

Use a Tier System: scale in/out instead of all-in/all-out and adjust exposure based on confirmation.

*

Relative Strength = Where The Money Is

If $SPY falls into the gap, but your stock stays above its gap – that stock is showing relative strength.

And that’s where institutions are holding and where you want to stay long or add.

*

So, the market just had a powerful technical rally. Now it’s in the decision phase. Everything comes down to holding vs losing the gap levels.

Don’t predict, react to the gap behavior, focus on relative strength names, and manage positions actively.
